---
title: "Regression Activity - Pt. 2: Regression Modeling"
crossref:
  lst-title: "🎯 Checkpoint 5."
  lst-labels: alpha a
format: live-html
sidebar: lesson-3
---

## Regression Modeling

<br>
<div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 56.25%; height: 0; overflow: hidden; max-width: 100%;">
  <iframe 
    src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/e18KM681cmg?start=8&end=74" 
    frameborder="0" 
    allowfullscreen
    style="position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; border: 0;">
  </iframe>
</div>
<br>

There are a few ways to create a line of best fit, but we will use Python to run a linear regression for us! It will generate our line of best fit.

:::{.dkorange-container}
Replace the `_` placeholder with the color values and nitrate concentrations from the RBG activity and scatterplot!

**IMPORTANT:** The color values and nitrate concentrations must be in the exact same order.

Click the ▶️ Run Code button to run the block and store your data.
:::

<!-- ```{pyodide}
#| include: false
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from plotnine import ggplot, aes, geom_point, geom_line, labs, theme_minimal, annotate

# Code that will convert lists to arrays.
def process_data(color_data, nitrate_ppm):
    color_data = np.array(color_data)
    nitrate_ppm = np.array(nitrate_ppm)
    return color_data, nitrate_ppm

# Creates dataframe for plotnine
def process_df(color_data, nitrate_ppm):
    df = pd.DataFrame({
        "color_data": color_data,
        "nitrate_ppm": nitrate_ppm
    })
    return df

# Runs a linear regression (looks better than NumPy function)
def linear_regression(x, y):
    slope, intercept = np.polyfit(x, y, 1)
    return slope, intercept
``` -->


```{pyodide}
#| persist: true
#| completion: false
color_data = [_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _]

nitrate_ppm = [_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _]

print(color_data)
print(nitrate_ppm)

# Do not change the code below ------------------------------------
color_data = np.array(color_data)
nitrate_ppm = np.array(nitrate_ppm)
df = pd.DataFrame({
        "color_data": color_data,
        "nitrate_ppm": nitrate_ppm
    })
```

### Drawing Your Line of Best Fit
:::{.gray-container}
**Materials Needed:**  
- Poster Markers  
- Scatterplot   
:::

:::{.dkorange-container}
Replace the `_` placeholders with the x variable in the first blank and the y variable in the second.

Click the ▶️ Run Code button to create a scatterplot that should match your group's paper plot.
:::

```{pyodide}
#| persist: true
#| completion: false
plot = (
    ggplot(df, aes(x="_", y="_"))
    + geom_point(size=3, color="black")
    + labs(
        title="Nitrate vs. Color",
        x="Color",
        y="Nitrate (ppm)"
    )+
    theme_minimal()
)
print(plot)
```

:::{.dkorange-container}
Replace the `__` placeholders with the x variable in the first blank and the y variable in the second.

Click the ▶️ Run Code button to run the block and generate the equation for the line of best fit.
:::

```{pyodide}
#| persist: true
#| completion: false

slope, intercept = np.polyfit(_, _, 1)
```

```{pyodide}
plot = (
    ggplot(df, aes(x="color_data", y="nitrate_ppm"))
    + geom_point(size=3, color="black")
    + geom_line(aes(y="nitrate_pred"), color="tomato", size=1)
    + labs(
        title="Nitrate vs. Color",
        x="Color",
        y="Nitrate (ppm)"
    )
    + annotate(
        "text",
        x=min(color_data),
        y=max(nitrate_ppm),
        label=f"y = {slope:.2f}x + {intercept:.2f}\nR² = {r_squared:.4f}",
        ha="left",
        size=10,
        color="gray"
    )
    + theme_minimal()
)

print(plot)
```

### Making Predictions
:::{.gray-container}
**Materials Needed:**  
- [RGB Applet V2](https://appalachianaicorps.org/modules/water-quality/RGB-Applet-2.html)  
- Three Additional "Nitrate Test Pad" Cards  
- Pencil   
:::
